Title: Rescuers search for missing in China storms after 100,000 flee
Article:
LIULAN: Rescuers scoured flooded parts of China for survivors on Wednesday after storms killed 17 people and caused dozens of rivers to overflow and a reservoir dam to burst, with officials warning of more rain.
Extreme weather has wreaked havoc on the country's south and centre this week, with a super typhoon heading towards eastern provinces this weekend.
In the southern region of Guangxi, six people died and at least 130,000 people were moved to safety after torrential rain and severe flooding from Typhoon Maysak, regional officials said, warning rain would continue to fall there and in neighbouring Guangdong province on Wednesday.
Fast-flowing muddy water burst the banks of 40 rivers and waterways in Guangxi, damaging nearly 13,000 acres of agricultural land, state media reported.
One hotel employee in the city of Guigang told AFP that over the past two days, the water level had risen to the height of a one-storey house, and that residents had to evacuate to hotels or to relatives' houses elsewhere.
While the water had now receded, they said there was still over a metre of standing water in low-lying places such as underpasses.
Videos published by state broadcaster CCTV showed torrents of water rushing past the crumbled concrete walls of a reservoir dam that had burst, while rescue workers wearing life vests were deployed on inflatable boats.
Chinese authorities were sending additional disaster relief supplies, including food, raincoats and rubber boats, to the region, state news agency Xinhua reported on Wednesday.
Authorities in Guangxi have maintained the second-highest level for flood-control emergency response, according to the State Flood Control and Drought Relief Headquarters.
Flood peaks "exceeding the warning water level" by more than six metres (20 feet) are expected at the Wuzhou Hydrological Station in Guangxi early Thursday, Minister of Water Resources Li Guoying said.
"Due to the impact of persistent heavy rainfall and the prolonged passage of floodwaters at high levels, the safety of reservoirs and embankments in the affected areas faces a severe test," he added.
In the central province of Hubei, thunderstorms and gale-force winds have killed 11 people and injured 331, and tornadoes were reported elsewhere late on Monday, Xinhua said.
One person is missing in Hubei, Xinhua said, adding that 4,800 houses were damaged and 22 more had collapsed.
China's President Xi Jinping said on Tuesday that rescuers should "go all out" in organising emergency operations, CCTV reported.
[[nid:1483526]]
TYPHOON 'COMBAT READINESS'
Eastern provinces are meanwhile preparing for the impact of Super Typhoon Bavi, which could make landfall "near the Zhejiang-Fujian border area" between Saturday and Sunday, said Xiang Chunyi, Chief Typhoon Forecaster at the National Meteorological Center.
"Alternatively, it may move northward in the waters east of Taiwan and make landfall directly on the coast of Zhejiang," Xiang told CCTV.
Taiwan, Zhejiang and Fujian will be the first to experience heavy rainfall, she added.
Bavi earlier this week tore through the US Pacific territories, leaving tens of thousands of people without power on Tuesday on Guam and the Northern Marianas.
"Bavi is characterised by high intensity and substantial energy, and it has maintained super typhoon status for a prolonged period," Xiang said.
The Party Secretary of Zhejiang's Hangzhou city, Liu Fei, urged officials to shift into a "state of combat readiness" to prepare for Bavi's arrival.
Jiangsu province, also in China's eastern region, said it would suspend "all in-person educational activities" at schools from Thursday afternoon through Friday and urged students and teachers to "pay attention to typhoon safety."
Natural disasters are common across China, particularly in the summer when some regions experience intense rainfall while others bake in scorching heat.
But scientists warn the intensity and frequency of global extreme weather events will increase as the planet continues to heat up because of fossil fuel emissions.
LANDSLIDE
Separately in northwestern China's Gansu province, the death toll from a landslide rose to 21, state media reported on Wednesday, a day after it buried 33 people.
Rescue teams had rushed to the site of the landslide, which occurred at around 6.56 am on Tuesday (2256 GMT Monday) at Rencang village in Dangchang County, Xinhua said.
"Search and rescue operations at the site of the landslide in Dangchang County have concluded; the landslide resulted in 21 fatalities," Xinhua said on Wednesday, citing local authorities.
The cause of the landslide was still under investigation, according to local media.
Authorities have set aside 30 million yuan (US$4.4 million) in reconstruction funds following the landslide.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Iran targets sites in Bahrain, Kuwait after wave of US strikes
Article:
TEHERAN: Iran's Revolutionary Guards said they targeted US military sites in Bahrain and Kuwait on Wednesday after the US launched a wave of military strikes on Iran in response to attacks on tankers in the Strait of Hormuz.
In the latest blow to the fragile ceasefire agreement, the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps said it carried out a joint missile and drone operation against key US military sites in Bandar Salman, Bahrain's Fifth Naval District and Ali Al Salem Air Base in Kuwait, and shot down a US MQ-9 drone attempting to interfere in the operation.
Air raid sirens sounded in Bahrain and Kuwait, and the Kuwaiti army said air defences were confronting "hostile" missile and drone attacks.
There was no immediate comment on the strikes from the US military.
The US earlier unleashed fresh military strikes and revoked a licence allowing Iran to sell oil in response to attacks on three tankers in the strait.
The US Central Command said more than 60 small boats of the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps were among the targets hit during the operation, which was intended to impose a heavy cost on Iran for strikes on shipping in violation of the ceasefire.
"The unwarranted aggression by Iranian forces is a clear and dangerous violation of the ceasefire and undermines freedom of navigation," Centcom said in a statement.
Nato Secretary General Mark Rutte told reporters before a summit of NATO leaders in Ankara that the new attacks by the US on Iran were "absolutely necessary".
"When you have a ceasefire and Iran is basically violating the ceasefire, I think it is totally crucial that the US forcefully react," Rutte said.
Iran's top joint military command, Khatam al-Anbiya Central Headquarters, condemned the US strikes as a "blatant act of aggression", threatened a "crushing response", and warned that Teheran would not allow US interference in the management of the strait.
A top Iranian negotiator, parliament speaker Mohammad Baqer Qalibaf, accused the US of breaching the ceasefire agreement.
He cited not only the latest US military strikes, but renewed oil sanctions, violations of Iranian "adjustments" in the Strait of Hormuz, and Israeli attacks against Lebanon.
"The era of bullying and extortion is over," Qalibaf said in a post on X. "We don't fold."
Iranian media earlier reported explosions in Iran's main oil hub of Kharg Island, on Qeshm Island and in the southern port cities of Sirik and Bandar Abbas.
Iran's Press TV reported several blasts were heard in southern Kharg Island. Centcom made no mention of Kharg Island, from which Iran exports 90 per cent of its crude oil.
A US official told Reuters that strikes targeted Iranian air defence systems, coastal surveillance systems, surface-to-air missiles, anti-ship cruise missiles and drone launch sites.
No civilian deaths were reported in Iran, but several people were injured by shrapnel from an "enemy projectile" that hit a commercial pier in Sirik, according to an Iranian state TV reporter.
The reports said strikes also hit fishing piers in Sirik and in Bandar Abbas.
The incidents were the latest threat to the fragile ceasefire agreement the US and Iran struck last month, pausing the conflict that started with US and Israeli strikes across the Islamic Republic.
OIL PRICES RISE
In a potentially major blow to that agreement, Washington moved on Tuesday to withdraw a key concession that had allowed Iran to sell oil on international markets.
Oil prices rose more than 3 per cent after the US announced the move.
A US official said earlier that negotiators continued to work in good faith towards a final agreement with Iran.
But control of the strait has given Teheran immense leverage, effectively allowing it to force a stalemate with the world's most powerful military.
Analysts say Teheran uses attacks on ships to underscore that leverage as it negotiates a long-term peace deal with the US.
Under the interim US-Iran agreement, the US Treasury issued a June 22 general licence to allow the sale of crude oil and petrochemical and petroleum products of Iranian origin through Aug 21.
In revoking that licence on Tuesday, it gave Iran until July 17 to wind down any transactions.
ANY MEASURE NECESSARY
Iran's foreign ministry condemned the move as a breach of the framework agreement to end the war and said Washington would bear responsibility for the consequences.
The ministry said early on Wednesday that Iran would take any measure it deemed necessary to safeguard its interests and national security.
While Teheran denied responsibility for the latest strikes on ships in the strait, Qatar blamed Iran for attacking the vessels, including the huge Qatari liquefied natural gas tanker, the Al Rekayyat, which reported being struck by a drone that caused a fire in its engine room.
The crew were safe and being evacuated.
A Saudi-flagged crude oil tanker, believed to be the supertanker Wedyan, was also damaged off Oman, maritime security sources said.
The cause was not immediately clear.
Iran's foreign ministry said Qatar's accusations were perplexing and that Teheran was diligently fulfilling its commitments.
Even so, it said commercial vessels faced risks for using routes not coordinated with Iran.
A second US official, speaking on condition of anonymity, said initial indications were that Iran had fired at three commercial vessels.
Iran's clerical rulers aim to install a permanent system to collect fees in what would amount to a huge shift in the balance of power in a region where Washington has long acted as guarantor of security.
The US strikes came after huge crowds mourned Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei in the holy city of Qom.
Khamenei was killed with his daughter, granddaughter, son-in-law and daughter-in-law on the war's first day.
The ceasefire was intended to provide a 60-day window for negotiations on a permanent agreement, but indirect talks in Qatar ended last week with no sign of headway.
US President Donald Trump has repeatedly threatened to resume bombing unless Iran agrees to "make a deal".
Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi said that under the terms of the interim ceasefire memorandum, negotiations on the final deal would "not commence if threats continue."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: OpenAI to unveil GPT-5.6 on Thursday after delaying launch
Article:
OpenAI will publicly launch its most capable model, GPT-5.6, on Thursday, after delaying the launch last month on the US government's request amid national security concerns that powerful AI systems could be misused.
This comes on the heels of the US government lifting curbs on Anthropic's latest Fable and Mythos AI models last week, less than three weeks after the company was ordered to suspend their access over national security risks.
Washington has increased scrutiny of advanced AI model releases to identify potential threats on concerns that the technology could be misused by military or intelligence in China, Russia or other countries of concern.
Axios, which broke the news on the OpenAI launch, reported citing a source familiar with the matter, that the US Department of Commerce had approved a broad launch of GPT-5.6, following additional government testing under Washington's new oversight framework for frontier AI.
OpenAI had limited the model's access to a small group of vetted partners whose details were shared with the authorities.
The tech firm now plans to launch GPT-5.6 Sol, along with Terra and Luna models, OpenAI said in a post on X late on Tuesday.
Sol is OpenAI's most advanced model yet, while Terra is the mid-tier lower-cost model and Luna is the most cost-efficient option.
The White House and the US Department of Commerce did not immediately respond to a Reuters request for comment outside regular business hours.
Increased scrutiny of AI models began with US President Donald Trump signing an executive order establishing a voluntary framework for AI developers to offer "covered frontier models" to ​the US government for up to 30 days before releasing them to trusted partners.
Anthropic has warned that it was "probably impossible" to make any AI model fully robust to jailbreaks and noted the potential for the development of a universal jailbreak that would be able to unblock "an entire class of harmful behaviors."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: A1 AK Koh to buy RM16.7mil land in Puchong Jaya for regional expansion
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: ACE Market-listed A1 AK Koh Group Bhd has proposed to acquire a 99-year leasehold parcel of land in Puchong Jaya, Selangor, for RM16.73 million to develop a new regional sales and marketing office.
In a circular to shareholders filed on Bursa Malaysia, the company said its subsidiary AK Koh Enterprise Sdn Bhd, will acquire the 4,089.85 square metres vacant commercial land from Qualitypack Properties Sdn Bhd.
The purchase will be funded through a combination of bank borrowings and internally generated funds.
The proposed acquisition is subject to shareholders' approval and is expected to be completed by the fourth quarter of 2026.
According to the company, the land has a market value of RM16.5 million based on an independent valuation dated April 9, 2026.
The agreed purchase price represents a premium of RM228,740 or 1.39 per cent, which the board said is justified by the site's strategic location and availability.
A1 plans to develop a four-storey commercial building with an estimated gross floor area of about 45,260 square feet at an indicative gross development cost of RM11.03 million.
The company expects to finalise the comprehensive development plan in the first quarter of 2027 before applying for the necessary approvals.
Preparatory works and regulatory approvals are expected to take between eight and 12 months.
A1 said the new building will replace its current rented office in Taman Perindustrian OUG, Kuala Lumpur, which has a built-up area of about 4,938 square feet and incurs a monthly rental of RM10,000.
The group said revenue had grown by about six per cent between the financial years ended June 30, 2022 and June 30, 2025, driving the need for larger premises to support its expanding operations in the central region.
The company also said the new facility would support its plans to expand into new market segments, including halal and horeca (hotel, restaurant and catering), while strengthening sales, marketing and customer engagement in Peninsular Malaysia's central region.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Myanmar man jailed for misappropriating nearly 6,000 litres of diesel
Article:
BUTTERWORTH: A Myanmar national was sentenced to six months' jail by the Sessions Court here after pleading guilty to misappropriating 5,808 litres of controlled diesel without a valid licence.
Nai Thet Naung Oo, 38, admitted to the charge after it was read to him before judge Roslan Hamid.
He committed the offence at an unnumbered store in the Sungai Lokan industrial area on the evening of April 15.
The charge was framed under Section 20(1) of the Control of Supplies Act, which makes it illegal to move, remove or transfer controlled and subsidised goods from licensed premises without written permission from the Supply Controller.
Deputy public prosecutor Preeya Darrsini Sugumaran prosecuted, assisted by prosecuting officer Mohd Hilmi Abu Hassan.
Roslan ordered the sentence to run from the date of Nai's arrest.
Meanwhile, state Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry director S. Jegan said there would be no compromise on any form of manipulation involving controlled goods or leakage of subsidised items.
"We urge the public with information on smuggling or misappropriation of controlled and subsidised goods to lodge reports through the ministry's official complaint channels so that necessary action can be taken," he said in a statement.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor election: BN targets fence-sitters in final campaign push
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Barisan Nasional will focus on winning over fence-sitters during the final week of campaigning for the Johor election.
Umno secretary-general Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki said the strategy would be the coalition's main priority as it seeks to engage every voter before polling day on Saturday.
"We will focus on fence-sitters as well as those who are not considered our supporters.
"We want to ensure that no voter is left untouched — no stone unturned. Even those regarded as staunch supporters of the opposition may ultimately have a change of heart.
"What matters is that we continue making the effort, persuading them and presenting what we have to offer," he told reporters after attending the launching of the Johor Entrepreneurs Gathering at the Persada Johor International Convention Centre here today.
Asyraf Wajdi said all BN election machinery had also been reminded to conduct their campaigns in a civil and respectful manner by refraining from abusive language and the spread of slander.
"I have reminded all our machinery to uphold good conduct and proper decorum. Do not resort to abusive language or spread slander because BN's approach has always been to campaign in a courteous and respectful manner," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Pekat Group secures two data centre infrastructure jobs worth RM46.9mil
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Pekat Group Bhd has secured two contracts totalling RM46.94 million to supply and install earthing and lightning protection systems for data centre developments in Negri Sembilan and Johor.
The first contract, valued at RM19.73 million, is for a hyperscale data centre in Port Dickson, Negeri Sembilan, and is scheduled for completion by June 30, 2027.
The second contract, worth RM27.21 million, involves the supply and installation of earthing and lightning protection systems for a data centre in Johor, with completion targeted by Sept 19, 2027.
Pekat group chief executive officer Tai Yee Chee said the latest contract underscores clients' confidence in Pekat's technical expertise and execution capabilities.
"Earthing and lightning protection systems are critical safety infrastructure for data centres, given the high-value equipment and continuous uptime requirements involved.
"These contract wins reflect the trust our clients place in our technical expertise and track record of delivery, and they further strengthen our order book in the data centre segment," he said.
Pekat said the contracts are expected to contribute positively to the group's earnings for the current financial year, subject to the progress of works and normal project execution risks.
The company has been strengthening its position in the data centre supply chain and aims to secure more contracts as demand for resilient data centre infrastructure continues to grow.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: World Cup screening ends in brawl at Johor restaurant
Article:
JOHOR BARU: A brawl involving a group of football fans watching the FIFA World Cup at a nasi kandar restaurant in Taman Universiti here early today has gone viral on social media.
A restaurant employee, who declined to be named, said the incident was the first of its kind in the 10 years the eatery has been operating.
"The fight is believed to have been triggered by developments during the match. It was unexpected and beyond our control.
"There was no significant damage to the restaurant or financial loss. None of our staff at the scene was injured.
"However, we were informed that a car was damaged, although we are unsure whether it belonged to one of our customers," the employee said when contacted.
The employee said the situation was eventually brought under control and did not escalate into a more serious incident.
"The incident was captured by the restaurant's closed-circuit television (CCTV) cameras, and the footage has been handed over to the authorities," the employee added.
Meanwhile, a spokesman for the Johor Baru Utara district police headquarters said investigations are ongoing.
Several videos shared on Threads showed football fans hurling chairs while the match was being screened at the restaurant.
Comments on the posts by people claiming to have witnessed the incident alleged that the fight broke out after Argentina scored two goals.
One supporter said he had gone to the restaurant to watch the match but was caught off guard by the commotion.
Egypt was eliminated from the FIFA World Cup after suffering a dramatic 3-2 defeat to Argentina, led by Lionel Messi, in the Round of 16 early today.
Despite the defeat, Egypt made history by advancing beyond the group stage and reaching the knockout rounds for the first time in the country's World Cup history.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Penang police to dispose of RM1.1mil in obscene film VCDs, DVDs
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: Police will dispose of VCDs and DVDs containing obscene films worth an estimated RM1.1 million at the northeast district police headquarters this Friday.
Penang police chief Datuk Dennis Lim said the items formed the bulk of seized goods worth RM1.28 million linked to 565 cases.
He said the seized items were from probes into contraband, vice and gambling cases between 2010 and 2017.
Other items to be disposed of include alcoholic beverages, cigarettes and entertainment equipment.
"The disposal of case items is an important part of investigation management as it ensures evidence stored remains orderly and secure while preventing misuse," Lim said in a statement.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor election: 'Onn tak Onn' phenomenon reflects growing acceptance of Onn Hafiz
Article:
JOHOR BARU: The 'Onn tak Onn' phenomenon seen throughout the Johor election campaign reflects the public's growing acceptance of Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi's leadership as Johor Menteri Besar, said Umno secretary-general Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki.
He said the trend also signalled that voters were familiar with Onn Hafiz as a leader and recognised that Barisan Nasional candidates were not merely making promises during the campaign.
"Wherever you go, this 'Onn tak Onn' phenomenon is real. It shows that people know the Menteri Besar and his executive councillors, about two-thirds of whom are under the age of 45.
"They have shown extraordinary commitment because they have consistently engaged with the people. They did not only come down to the ground during the campaign to make promises.
"Our campaign is simple. Don't believe promises, look at the track record. That is what matters most," he said after officiating the Johor Entrepreneurs Gathering at the Persada Johor International Convention Centre here today.
He was responding to a question on whether the participation of Parti Bersama Malaysia candidates in Umno-held state constituencies would affect BN's support in the Johor election.
[[nid:1483496]]
Asyraf, who is also Majlis Amanah Rakyat (Mara) chairman, remained confident in BN Johor's strength in then polls.
"The people of Johor have been with BN for a long time. They have experienced what it is like to be governed by another coalition and eventually returned to BN in 2022," he said.
Asyraf said BN was increasingly confident of securing victory, citing growing momentum on the campaign trail.
"Alhamdulillah, I have travelled to nearly two-thirds of the state constituencies across Johor. I can see the momentum continuing to build, and what excites me most is the support from non-Malay voters, especially the Chinese and Indian communities.
"They have welcomed our candidates and leaders. This time, our campaign has focused on volunteers from various levels going door to door to meet voters, and that approach has proven to be more effective.
"That is why we have not organised many mega ceramah or large public rallies. Instead, we are focusing on meeting as many voters as possible. Alhamdulillah, during the first week, our volunteers managed to visit almost every house, while the second week has been about reinforcing those engagements," he said.
He said BN's focus during the final week of campaigning would be on fence-sitters and voters who were not traditional supporters of the coalition.
Asyraf also reminded BN's election machinery to maintain proper conduct throughout the campaign and avoid abusive language or spreading slander.
"BN has always conducted its campaign in a civil and respectful manner," he said.
Ahead of polling day on Saturday, Asyraf urged Johoreans to fulfil their civic duty and give BN a mandate if they wanted Onn Hafiz's development agenda to continue.
"We are seeking the people's mandate not to secure anyone's release, nor out of greed, arrogance or pride, but because he (Onn Hafiz) has outlined a major agenda to realise Johor Maju 2030.
"The people will decide. If they believe in this vision and are prepared to embrace it, then they should vote for BN," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: AirBorneo to rationalise rural routes as it strengthens reliability
Article:
KUCHING: AirBorneo Airways Sdn Bhd will rationalise selected rural air services (RAS) frequencies as part of efforts to improve reliability and aircraft serviceability, chief executive officer Megat Ardian Aminuddin said.
He said the airline was focusing on operational resiliency, maintenance support, network rationalisation and long-term fleet renewal after taking over the RAS operation from MASwings Sdn Bhd on Jan 1, 2026.
AirBorneo currently operates 14 dedicated turboprop aircraft for RAS, comprising eight ATR 72-500 aircraft and six Twin Otters.
The aircraft serve intra-Sarawak, intra-Sabah and Labuan routes connecting rural communities under the public service obligation network.
Megat Ardian said there had been disruptions in the RAS network and AirBorneo is working with maintenance providers and spare parts suppliers to improve aircraft turnaround.
He said the airline was focusing on faster spare parts transfer, deploying the right technical resources to where aircraft are located, improving turnaround during checks and repairs, and engaging new suppliers.
"It is about the speed of fixing things. It is about the speed of getting the aircraft back into the schedule," he said during a media briefing here today.
Megat Ardian said AirBorneo was also reviewing its RAS network by merging some low-load frequencies while protecting high demand services.
He said the move would allow the airline to allocate more time for aircraft serviceability.
"Network rationalisation where we are trying to allocate more time for service. That is where we are merging some flights, rationalising the network and cutting some frequencies on flights that have low loads."
"Sometimes out of 70 or 68 seats, sometimes only half get filled. Sometimes on our Twin Otters, we have nine seats, only two get filled. So some of these we have to rationalise, we have to merge them," he said.
Megat Ardian said high-demand frequencies would be maintained.
"We keep and we protect the high demand frequencies. High demand flights are always there.
"That is where we have sometimes 75 per cent load factors. Those are the flights that we keep. We do not touch those," he said.
He added that AirBorneo would announce a revised RAS schedule in the coming days after finalising details with the Transport Ministry.
"It will not close, but it will reduce," he said, adding that some routes could be merged from 14 times weekly to seven times weekly depending on demand.
Megat Ardian said AirBorneo carried about 60,000 to 70,000 RAS passengers per month from January and was targeting about 850,000 passengers annually.
He said the RAS operation was not only about tourism, but also essential connectivity for local communities in the interior.
"Because RAS is a social obligation, it is not just about tourism. It is about connecting cities to the interior as well, connecting transport for local communities there," he said.
Megat Ardian said the RM209 million annual subsidy from the federal government was specifically for RAS and did not cover AirBorneo's commercial jet operations.
"Yes, those subsidies are there, but those subsidies are specifically and only for the Rural Air Services. The jet operations are completely separate," he said.
He said AirBorneo was also discussing network rationalisation with the federal government with a new agreement expected to be finalised by the end of the year.
As part of its long-term RAS fleet renewal, AirBorneo has signed for eight new ATR 72-600s in February this year.
Megat Ardian said two aircraft would be delivered in 2027, four in 2028 and the remaining two in 2029.
He said the new ATR 72-600s would replace the existing ATR 72-500s on a one-for-one basis.
The airline is also starting commercial jet operations this month with two wet-leased Boeing B737-800s, beginning with the Kuching-Kuala Lumpur route.
Megat Ardian said starting with two aircraft would allow AirBorneo to maintain cost discipline and scale up gradually as more aircraft joined the fleet.
"Starting with two aircraft gives us a controlled growth trajectory. We start with two instead of starting with five or seven. With two, we can be very disciplined on cost," he said.
He said the airline would grow to five aircraft by the end of 2027, before adding one aircraft every quarter from 2028.
Megat Ardian said AirBorneo planned to move progressively from wet-leased aircraft to dry lease or financial lease aircraft from 2028, which would lower its cost base.
He said the airline was targeting 17 dry-leased aircraft by the end of its five-year fleet plan.
AirBorneo is also looking at cargo as an ancillary revenue stream as its jet fleet expands.
Megat Ardian said the airline did not plan to operate dedicated freighters, but would use belly cargo space on its passenger aircraft.
"Cargo is not a cargo operation per se. We will not have our own cargo aircraft. It is all belly space," he said.
He said AirBorneo would work with multiple cargo general sales agents instead of relying on one cargo player.
"Sarawak is a big region, and it can move cargo back and forth, not just to Peninsular Malaysia, but also to some international destinations. Cargo, yes, is a very important ancillary revenue for us to put forward," he said.
AirBorneo will also begin with interline partnerships before moving into deeper cooperation such as codeshares and, eventually, possible alliance membership.
He said the airline had already received interest from other airlines for interline and codeshare cooperation.
"We always will start off with interline first, and then progressively from the interline we work on some other deeper partnerships, which is codeshare. Then from codeshare also, we go up another level to joining an alliance," he said.
Megat Ardian said alliance membership would take time and could come at a later stage, possibly from year three to year five onwards, depending on AirBorneo's network, reliability and passenger profile.
He said other airlines would first want to see AirBorneo's operational performance, loads and service reliability.
"We may start interline as early as quarter four this year or quarter one next year," he said.
On profitability, Megat Ardian said the airline industry required scale, and AirBorneo would need more aircraft, passengers and network depth before achieving stronger cost efficiency.
"The airline industry is about scale. It is really about scale. With two aircraft, with five aircraft, even with seven aircraft, you do not get that much scale this year," he said.
Megat Ardian said AirBorneo would see more scale when its fleet reached the teens.
"I am not saying that we only break even when we are at 13 or 14 aircraft, but I am saying that is where we see a lot more scale and a lot more cost reduction as well," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: RM375 Kuching-KL fare to stabilise market, says AirBorneo
Article:
KUCHING: AirBorneo Airways Sdn Bhd (AirBorneo) says its RM375 all-in one-way economy class fare for Kuching-Kuala Lumpur route is aimed at stabilising airfares between Sarawak and Peninsular Malaysia as the state-owned airline prepares to begin jet operations this month.
AirBorneo chief executive officer (CEO) Megat Ardian Aminuddin said the fare was introduced after the airline benchmarked prices offered by other carriers on the route, including average lower fares recorded over the past six months and last year.
He said the RM375 fare included airport taxes, meals, baggage and seat selection.
"Ours is all-in. RM375 is what you see, what you pay. What you get on board is of value. So that is the difference that we have," he said during a media question and answer here today.
AirBorneo opened ticket sales for the route yesterday at 7am, offering all-in one-way economy class fares from RM375 and business class fares from RM736 under its "Sarawakku Sayang" promotion.
The airline is scheduled to start its inaugural commercial service from Kuala Lumpur to Kuching on July 20, 2026 while regular twice-daily Kuching-Kuala Lumpur services will begin from July 21, 2026.
For comparison, Utusan Sarawak reported that fare checks for the Kuching-Kuala Lumpur route showed Malaysia Airlines Bhd and AirBorneo offering fares from RM374.88 with checked baggage and meals included, while AirAsia offered fares from RM269 as a base fare with checked baggage, meals and seat selection charged separately.
Batik Air's Super Saver fare was listed from RM318.72 also without checked baggage and meals.
Megat Ardian said the fare was also part of AirBorneo's role in addressing high airfares between Sarawak and Peninsular Malaysia.
"One of the main things was the high fare price from Semenanjung and here. Those are the things that we want to stabilise going forward. That is why we introduced this fare to stabilise fares going forward," he said.
He added that the travel period for the fare runs until March 27, 2027 while the booking period would be assessed based on market response.
"The booking period is where we want to test the market and see how the market reacts."
"So far, what we have had yesterday and today has been positive. There are quite a lot of people who are excited also on the fare," he said.
Megat Ardian said the data would be important for AirBorneo to chart its future plans for the Kuching-Kuala Lumpur route.
He said AirBorneo's entry had already triggered a market response from other carriers.
"I think if local carriers did not react to our price, then it is irrelevant anyway. But it is good that we make an impact," he said, adding that the airline would continue to monitor the route's performance, competitor pricing and how long other carriers maintain their lower fares.
Business Times reported yesterday that AirBorneo was understood to be wet leasing two Boeing B737-800 aircraft from Batik Air for its planned jet services from Kuching to Kuala Lumpur.
The wet lease is for 18 months and could be extended if needed, sources said. A wet lease typically includes aircraft, crew, maintenance and insurance.
Megat Ardian said told the media briefing that AirBorneo would start its jet operations with two wet-leased B737-800 but he did not share information about the lessor.
Business Times first reported on Monday that AirBorneo had filed planned B737-800 jet operations in the OAG airline schedules database for Kuching-Kuala Lumpur and Kuching-Singapore Changi services from July 2026.
The filing showed Kuching-Kuala Lumpur services starting from July 20, while Kuching-Singapore services were planned from July 27 at the earliest.
Megat Ardian said AirBorneo has secured approval and air traffic rights for the Singapore route and was finalising the last stages of operational testing with Changi Airport.
He said the Singapore service would begin soon after the Kuala Lumpur route but did not indicate the exact launch date except saying that it could happen by end-July if everything went well.
Megat Ardian also said AirBorneo would start its jet operations with two aircraft this year before expanding to five aircraft by end-2027.
He said the airline planned to add more domestic and regional destinations as more aircraft joined the fleet.
"When we have additional aircraft, that is when we launch new routes," he said.
Megat Ardian said AirBorneo would target domestic and regional routes, including intra-Sarawak destinations such as Miri, Bintulu and Sibu, as well as points in Sabah and Asean cities.
He said regional routes would cover destinations within a three-to-five-hour flying radius from Sarawak.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Workshop owner charged over parang attack
Article:
SHAH ALAM: A workshop owner pleaded not guilty at the Sessions Court today to a charge of attacking a man with a parang last year.
K. Devaidran, 42, entered his plea after the charge was read to him in Tamil before judge 'Ainul Bashiirah Dona Don Biyajid.
Dressed in an orange T-shirt and jeans, he was accused of intentionally causing grievous hurt to B. Kartigeyen, 39, by slashing his body, legs and hands with a parang.
"I claim trial," he said while standing handcuffed in the dock.
The alleged offence was committed at 11.30pm at Kampung Bunga Melur, Batu 14, Puchong on Jan 25 last year.
The charge under Section 326 of the Penal Code, carries a jail term up to 20 years, a fine or whipping upon conviction.
Deputy public prosecutor Amir Saifullah Mohammad Sallehuddin asked the court to set bail at RM20,000 with one surety.
He also requested additional conditions requiring the accused to report to the police station, refrain from interfering with prosecution witnesses, especially the victim, and surrender his passport to the court.
Defence lawyer Mohd Safwan Saleh appealed for a minimal bail.
He told the court that Devaidran is married with one child and that his wife is currently recovering from back surgery.
"As a result of this case, the accused's workshop had been forced to close, making it difficult for him to raise a high bail amount. He is also his family's sole breadwinner," he said.
After hearing both parties, 'Ainul Bashiirah Dona allowed bail of RM10,000 with one surety and allowed the prosecution's additional conditions.
The court fixed Aug 13 for mention.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Disabled senior citizen dies in Kuantan house fire
Article:
KUANTAN: A senior citizen with a disability (PwD) is believed to have died from smoke inhalation after a fire broke out at his double-storey house in Taman Seri Mahkota Jaya here today.
The remains of G. Vithalinggam, 66, who had both legs amputated, were found on a bed in the living room on the ground floor.
Pahang Fire and Rescue Department director Datuk Mohd Razam Taja Rahim said the Taman Tas Fire and Rescue Station received an emergency call about the incident at 9.30am before dispatching eight firefighters to the scene.
"Firefighters were initially informed that no one was inside the house, prompting them to force open the front door to bring the fire under control. The house was filled with thick smoke.
"After the fire was extinguished at 9.50am, firefighters discovered the victim on his bed in the living room. A medical team later confirmed that he had died," he said in a statement today.
Razam said firefighters only realised there was a victim inside the premises after they had put out the fire and opened the rear door to ventilate the house.
"The victim did not suffer any burn injuries and is believed to have died from smoke inhalation. He was alone at home during the fire," he said.
He said the house sustained about 15 per cent damage, and the department was investigating the cause of the fire and the extent of the losses.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PAC demands strict oversight, financial discipline for RM11.22bil LCS project
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Parliamentary Public Accounts Committee (PAC) has urged the government to enforce strict monitoring and financial discipline on the Littoral Combat Ship (LCS) project to ensure all five vessels are delivered within the capped budget of RM11.22 billion.
In its six recommendations, the committee also proposed that the Defence Ministry (Mindef) and the Royal Malaysian Navy (RMN) be required to submit written progress reports to the PAC every three months, without fail, beginning in May this year.
"(This is) to ensure that any delays or new issues are detected and addressed early, including the status of combat system integration testing, given that the April 2026 testing phase is critical.
"Mindef and the Finance Ministry must maintain strict financial discipline to ensure the total contract cost of the LCS project remains below the final ceiling of RM11.22 billion.
"Mindef must ensure that this project is successfully completed and all five ships delivered to the RMN in accordance with the established timeline and costs," PAC chairman Datuk Mas Ermieyati Samsudin said.
She added that the project's main contractor, Lumut Naval Shipyard Sdn Bhd (Lunas), must maintain sufficient warranty and contingency stocks for critical equipment, such as radar systems, to address supply chain vulnerabilities arising from international vendors failing to deliver parts on time.
This follows a June 23 hearing after Norway's decision to revoke the export licence for the Naval Strike Missile (NSM).
"The Norwegian government's decision to withdraw and cancel the export licence for the NSM guided missiles for the LCS project could impact the vessels' combat  capability, as they risk being delivered without a complete primary weapon system," she said, adding that Malaysia had already paid 95 per cent of the procurement cost.
The PAC also called on the government to strengthen mitigation and diplomatic efforts to secure a fair resolution for the country while protecting Malaysia's fiscal sovereignty.
The government should fully leverage the negotiation channels or, if necessary, initiate legal proceedings to seek adequate compensation from defaulting parties in accordance with the agreed contract terms, she added.
According to the committee's report, the project's overall physical progress stood at 75.73 per cent as of December last year, with the first vessel (LCS 1) at 82.9 per cent completion.
While LCS 1 successfully completed its first sea trials on Jan 28, reaching a speed of 7 knots, its delivery has been delayed by a further four months to December this year.
Delivery of LCS 2 is scheduled for August next year, while the timelines for LCS 3 to LCS 5 remain unchanged under the original contract, with delivery running up to April 2029.
The PAC attributed the latest delay to a technical audit by France's Naval Group, which mandated rework on nearly 4,000 items, including the addition of 50km of cabling for LCS 1.
To safeguard public funds, the PAC said the contract remained capped at a fixed price of RM11.22 billion, with Lunas fully absorbing all costs arising from rework or repairs to obsolete components.
The government has also transitioned to the Earned Value Method (EVM), ensuring payments are made only for verified completed physical work rather than under the old milestone payment system, which was vulnerable to overpayment.
Since the project was taken over by the Ministry of Finance Incorporated (MOF Inc), funds have been monitored weekly and strictly segregated from Lunas' other commercial activities, such as maintenance, repair and overhaul (MRO) services.
The PAC noted that Lunas was addressing equipment obsolescence through original equipment manufacturer (OEM) refurbishments at its own cost, with 170 major onboard systems – including gun-firing and shock-proof certifications – still requiring testing.
Additionally, the Covid-19 pandemic caused several European OEMs to go bankrupt, forcing Lunas to find alternative vendors and navigate time-consuming recertification processes.
Meanwhile, a 12-month intensive training programme has been established to prepare RMN crews to operate the ships' complex anti-submarine and stealth technologies.
On the legal front, the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) confirmed that criminal investigations remained active, while governance probes had been completed, with advice provided to Mindef in August 2023.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MATTA urges review of diesel subsidy exclusion for tourism transport operators
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Association of Tour and Travel Agents (MATTA) has urged the government to reconsider its decision to exclude licensed tourism transport operators from the diesel subsidy programme, warning that the move could affect Malaysia's tourism industry and travellers.
MATTA president Nigel Wong disputed the recent characterisation by Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan that diesel subsidies for tourism transport operators would only benefit foreign tourists, saying licensed tourism vehicles serve both domestic and international travellers.
He said tourism transport operators provide services for domestic holidaymakers, school groups, corporate events, incentive travel, religious pilgrimages, educational tours and community programmes.
"It is therefore incorrect to assume that diesel subsidies for tourism transport operators would benefit only foreign tourists.
"To say this subsidy only helps foreigners ignores the reality of how our tourism transport industry operates. This decision, if left unchanged, will raise fares for Malaysians and may even force them to consider utilising unlicensed service providers," he said in a statement today.
The removal of diesel subsidies would directly increase operating costs for tourism transport operators, with the additional costs inevitably passed on through higher fares and more expensive travel packages for both Malaysians and international visitors.
He said the move could undermine the affordability that drives domestic tourism and inbound arrivals as Malaysia accelerates its Visit Malaysia 2026 (VM2026) campaign.
More affordable tourism services would encourage Malaysians to explore destinations within the country while enhancing Malaysia's appeal to international visitors, generating multiplier effects across hotels, restaurants, attractions, retailers, transport operators and local communities.
He added that the long-term economic benefits generated by stronger tourism activity could potentially outweigh the cost of providing targeted diesel subsidies to licensed tourism transport operators.
Meanwhile, MATTA called on the Finance Ministry (MoF) to reconsider the exclusion of licensed tourism transport operators from the diesel subsidy programme, engage with the Ministry of Tourism, Arts and Culture (MOTAC) and industry stakeholders to develop a targeted and well-governed subsidy mechanism, and recognise tourism transport as a strategic enabler of domestic mobility and the tourism economy in support of VM2026.
The association said supporting licensed tourism transport operators should be viewed as an investment in Malaysia's economic growth, employment and long-term competitiveness rather than merely a cost.
MATTA's statement follows the MoF's recent decision to exclude tourism transport vehicles from the diesel subsidy programme, a move the association said was based on the view that extending the subsidy to tourism transport operators would primarily benefit foreign tourists. -- BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Private land used for key Sentul road being acquired, says KL mayor
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A privately owned plot of land through which a key road in Sentul passes is being acquired, with the process expected to be completed within four months, said Mayor Datuk Fadlun Mak Ujud.
Commenting on a road closure notice affecting Jalan Taman Datuk Senu and parts of Jalan Taman Datuk Senu 3, Fadlun said Kuala Lumpur City Hall was in the midst of acquiring the land.
Fadlun said the process, which involves DBKL working with the Lands and Mines Department, was already at the approval stage.
He added that discussions on the acquisition had started as early as February, and that it was now awaiting approval before gazettement and compensation could proceed.
"We cannot carry out the acquisition ourselves. It has to be done by the federal government," he told reporters after launching the Downtown KL Grants Programme.
"We are trying to expedite it. If possible, it can be resolved within three to four months, provided the landowner agrees to the compensation amount, as the valuation is determined by the Valuation and Property Services Department," he said.
Under a notice dated June 29, the landowners said the road was situated on privately owned land and had never been surrendered to any authority.
As such, any use of the road by the public after that date would be without the owners' permission.
The road closure has raised concerns among nearby residents as it serves as a key link between several residential areas in Sentul and Taman Datuk Senu.
It has also sparked widespread attention online over the traffic congestion expected to result from the closure.
Meanwhile, Fadlun urged the landowner to allow continued public access while the acquisition process was completed.
"I advise the landowner to be patient because the process is underway."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Mindef: LCS project reaches 78.1pct completion as of May 31
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Littoral Combat Ship (LCS) project has reached 78.1 per cent physical completion as of May 31, compared with the planned target of 85.21 per cent, representing a shortfall of 7.11 percentage points.
The Defence Ministry (Mindef) said the project, which involves the construction of naval assets for the Royal Malaysian Navy (RMN), had incurred total expenditure of RM8.457 billion, or 76.2 per cent of the project's overall value.
"The Norwegian government's decision to revoke the export licence for the Naval Strike Missile (NSM) system to Malaysia will not affect the delivery schedule of the LCS vessels, as guaranteed by Lumut Naval Shipyard (Lunas) to the Defence Ministry," the ministry said in a written parliamentary reply published on the parliament website.
The ministry was responding to a question from Ahmad Fadhli Shaari (PN-Pasir Mas), who sought an update on the LCS project, the total amount of public funds spent as of May 31, the additional funding required to complete the project, and the government's assurance that the first vessel would be delivered to the RMN by December.
Mindef said the LCS would retain its key capabilities, including anti-submarine warfare, anti-air warfare, electronic warfare, and a Combat Management System (CMS) that integrates these capabilities with the ship's radar and sensor systems.
"At the same time, the ministry, through the RMN, is taking all necessary measures to restore the surface-to-surface missile capability of the LCS fleet through the best available solution," it said.
Norway previously revoked the export licence for the NSM system to Malaysia, citing its national security interests.
Following the decision, Mindef issued a notice of demand worth about RM1 billion to the defence company involved.
Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin had earlier said the issue would not affect the delivery timeline for the RMN's LCS vessels, which are currently under construction.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Iraq's holy cities host funeral processions for Khamenei
Article:
NAJAF: Crowds thronged the streets of Najaf on Wednesday as the coffin of Iran's supreme leader Ali Khamenei moved through the holy city in a procession devoted to Iraq, home to Shia Islam's most sacred shrines.
Iran began six days of public funeral ceremonies for Khamenei on Saturday, including a dedicated day to neighbouring Iraq, a Shia powerhouse with close ties to Tehran.
The Islamic republic hopes the marathon ceremonies will project strength and unity after the Middle East war, which started with US-Israeli strikes that killed Khamenei and several relatives on February 28.
The procession in Najaf came as the United States and Iran renewed hostilities in the Strait of Hormuz, putting more pressure on a deal to end the war.
The US military said it had struck dozens of Iranian targets in response to Tehran's attacks on three ships in Hormuz, with Iran's Revolutionary Guards later saying they had hit US military facilities in Bahrain and Kuwait.
After a massive procession in Iran's holy city of Qom, Iraqi officials and senior politicians received the remains of Khamenei on Tuesday night at Najaf international airport in the presence of Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian and one of the late leader's sons.
Iraqi authorities declared Wednesday a public holiday, with procession ceremonies starting at at 6 am (0300 GMT) in Najaf.
A heavy security deployment was in place as the crowds swelled, with some people pushing close to touch Khamenei's coffin as it rode in the back of a truck en route to the shrine of Imam Ali, the Prophet Mohammed's son-in-law and the first Shia Imam.
At the shrine, dozens of clerics stood ready to pray over the coffin before it was carried on to the city of Karbala.
Khamenei's final burial will take place on Thursday in his hometown of Mashhad in northeast Iran.
His eldest son Mostafa Khamenei was present at the airport on Tuesday, but his successor Mojtaba Khamenei, named supreme leader shortly after his father's killing, has not appeared in public and has only communicated through written statements since his nomination.
Iraqi Mohammed al-Bayati, 30, who travelled for hours to Najaf, said it was "an opportunity not to be missed to participate in the funeral of the person who challenged the power of America and Israel."
Najaf is the main centre of Shia religious seminaries, and is also home to Grand Ayatollah Ali Sistani, Iraq's top Shia religious authority.
[[nid:1483462]]
Many senior Shia clerics have studied, taught or lived there, including Khamenei's predecessor Ayatollah Ruhollah Khomeini.
After Najaf, Khamenei's body will be flown to Karbala, about 60 kilometres north, for another procession that will culminate at the shrines of Imam Hussein and his brother Abbas.
The death of Hussein, the third Shia Imam, in the seventh century remains central to Shia history and draws millions of people from around the world to Karbala and Najaf every year.
In Karbala, one banner read "we bid you farewell" and another displayed Khamenei's photo with the caption, "the one who humiliated America."
In both cities, hundreds of volunteer-run stalls serving food and drinks to mourners lined the procession routes.
The bond between Iraq and neighbouring Iran, both Shia-majority countries, runs deep and is shaped by both religion and politics.
Iranian state media quoted Esmail Qaani, head of the Guards' Quds Force, as saying: "The extensive planning for this historical event by the Iraqi government and people show the depth of the spiritual bond between the two great nations of Iraq and Iran to the whole world."
Ties between Iran and Iraq were not always strong.
In the 1980s, Iraq's late ruler Saddam Hussein, who repressed the Shia population, went to war with the Islamic republic.
But the two countries have become close allies since Saddam's fall in 2003 in a US-led invasion and with the rise to power of Shia-dominated governments in Baghdad.
Today, Iran backs influential politicians but also armed groups, some of which joined the Middle East war after Khamenei's death in support of Iran, attacking US facilities in Iraq.
Haidar Jaafar, who travelled from the southern city of Basra to Najaf, said he expected millions of people to attend Khamenei's procession, "even those who do not align with Iran, just because he was killed by Israeli-American hands."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: KKIA expansion awaits resolution of land issues, says deputy minister
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The nearly RM500 million expansion project for Kota Kinabalu International Airport (KKIA) is expected to begin once land and site-related issues are resolved with the Sabah government, Deputy Transport Minister Datuk Hasbi Habibollah said.
He said funding for the project had already been approved, but several matters concerning land requirements, the expansion site and areas adjacent to the existing runway still needed to be finalised.
"As for the Tawau Airport expansion, works are already underway. For KKIA, once all land-related matters have been settled, the project should be ready to proceed. However, we are still awaiting the Sabah government's decision on details concerning the project site," he said.
He was responding to a supplementary question from Datuk Shahelmey Yahya (BN-Putatan) during Question Time in the Dewan Rakyat today on the status of the KKIA and Tawau Airport expansion projects announced under Budget 2026.
Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, who is also Finance Minister, announced during the tabling of Budget 2026 last October an allocation of RM2.3 billion for the development of airports in Penang, Kota Kinabalu, Tawau and Miri, with the projects expected to be completed by 2028.
Responding to Nordin Ahmad Ismail (PN-Lumut), who asked whether the government planned to upgrade Pangkor Airport or restore commercial flight services to the island, Hasbi said sea transport remained the preferred mode of travel for both tourists and residents.
He said the government welcomed any airline interested in operating services at the airport, adding that the Ministry of Transport (MOT) and Malaysia Airports Holdings Bhd (MAHB) remained open to proposals on the matter.
However, he said any decision to commence operations would depend on the airline's commercial assessment and business viability.
"Pangkor Airport previously handled charter services operated by Berjaya Air using Dash 7 aircraft and by SKS Airways from Sultan Abdul Aziz Shah Airport in Subang, but the services were discontinued in May 2022.
"The government recognises that air connectivity is a key driver of the tourism sector. Therefore, MOT will ensure that any future decision regarding the airport takes into account the potential growth in demand for air services," he said.
Hasbi also clarified that Pangkor Airport, as well as the airports on Redang Island and Tioman Island, had not been completely closed or left to become 'white elephants'.
Instead, all three airports continued to be used for private aircraft, military aircraft and helicopter operations, emergency landings, and flying doctor services, while MAHB personnel remained stationed there to maintain the airport assets.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Vietnam looks at more coal power plants as Iran war complicates LNG plans
Article:
HANOI: Vietnam's government is considering building more coal-fired power plants to ensure it can meet its energy needs, the government said on Wednesday, as the Iran war further complicates its slow development of LNG facilities.
The Southeast Asian manufacturing hub is seeking to ramp up its power generation capacity to support its fast-growing economy, with a focus on renewables and LNG.
"In recent times, the unfolding conflicts in the Middle East have impacted the security of LNG supplies, creating a need to strengthen energy security," the government said in a statement on Wednesday.
It said it may adjust its national power plan to add more coal-generated energy to the mix.
Vietnam had set a target to have a fleet of LNG-fired power plants with combined capacity of 22.5 gigawatts by 2030.
It has so far reached just 7.3 per cent of that target, due mostly to regulatory hurdles and lack of investor interest, though the Iran war has also raised concerns about the security of LNG supplies. Under its existing plan, Vietnam is aiming for total installed capacity in a range of 183 to 236 GW by 2030, with coal-fired power plants accounting for 13.1 per cent-16.9 per cent of the mix, and LNG for 9.5 per cent-12.3 per cent.
Electricity output in the first half of this year rose 9.8 per cent from a year earlier to 171.5 billion kilowatt hours, with coal-fired power plants accounting for 54.5 per cent of the output, according to state utility EVN.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Tiong urges collaboration to boost handicrafts overseas
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: State governments and private companies have been urged to collaborate with the Tourism, Arts and Culture Ministry to promote local products, including batik, in international markets.
Its minister, Datuk Seri Tiong King Sing, said such cooperation was important to give local products greater international exposure.
"Most importantly, when promoting products overseas, the states should work together with the government so that more people abroad are aware of what Pahang has to offer.
"I am asking the Yang Berhormat (Kuantan) that, if possible, we sit down later to discuss how we can work together to upgrade products from Pahang," he told the Dewan Rakyat today.
He was responding to Wan Razali Wan Nor (PN-Kuantan) on the ministry's efforts to assist batik industry players in the Kuantan parliamentary constituency, including support to increase production capacity, competitiveness and the marketing of local batik products.
Tiong said Kraftangan Malaysia currently provided extensive support to entrepreneurs in marketing local products through both physical and digital channels.
He said Kraftangan Malaysia also supported entrepreneurs through technical, entrepreneurship and marketing training, production laboratories, and the Community Skills Development Programme, which focuses on the B40 community, persons with disabilities and single mothers.
He said Kraftangan Malaysia also supported the development of new products that met market demand while providing promotional platforms through physical events such as the Craft Festival and digital platforms including MyCraftShoppe, E-Kraf Bazaar and Craft on the Go.
"Although Kraftangan Malaysia does not provide direct financial assistance, entrepreneurs may apply for the Craft Entrepreneur Financing Scheme, which offers financing of up to RM100,000 for the purchase of equipment and machinery, with a flexible repayment period of up to 10 years."
Tiong said the ministry and Kraftangan Malaysia would strengthen strategic cooperation with the private sector to upgrade handicraft centres into more modern, interactive and competitive hubs.
"This approach aims to increase the commercial value of local handicraft products, including batik, expand market access and create more export opportunities so that Malaysian handicraft products can penetrate international markets."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Israel must immediately release detained Gaza hospital chief: UN experts
Article:
GENEVA: United Nations experts demanded Tuesday that Israel immediately release a Gaza hospital director who has been held since December 2024 and who reportedly has suffered severe torture and life-threatening injuries.
Hussam Abu Safiya, a paediatrician and director of Gaza's Kamal Adwan hospital, rose to prominence in 2024 by posting about the dire conditions in his besieged hospital in Beit Lahia during a major Israeli offensive.
On Dec 27 that year, Israeli forces began an assault on the facility, labelling the only functioning hospital in northern Gaza at the time a Hamas "terrorist centre", and arresting dozens of medical staff including Abu Safiya.
"Dr. Abu Safiya's ongoing arbitrary detention without charges or trial is a reflection of Israel's systematic targeting of Palestinian health workers," four independent UN experts  said in a statement.
They also charged that Israel's "continuing decimation of the health care system in Gaza" was aimed "to inflict conditions to bring about the mental and physical destruction of Palestinians."
The experts, who are mandated by the UN Human Rights Council but who do not speak on behalf of the United Nations, said they had previously raised concerns with Israel about the legality of Israel's Unlawful Combatants' Law.
Pointing to reports that Abu Safiya had been subjected to "continued severe torture" and also that "his injuries could lead to imminent death", the experts insisted that Israel ensure he "has access to immediate and adequate medical care."
The experts also included the special rapporteur on the rights situation in the Palestinian territories Francesca Albanese, who has faced harsh Israel criticism and allegations of anti-Semitism from Israel amid her repeated accusations that Israel is committing "genocide" in Gaza.
They charged Tuesday that Israel during the war in Gaza had "turned the practice of medicine into a crime - and made health and care workers targets of harassment, intimidation, arrests, torture and death."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Rohingya man held over alleged rape of teenager in Kelantan
Article:
BACHOK: Police have arrested a 32-year-old Rohingya man suspected of raping a 19-year-old woman in Kampung Kuar, near Tawang, last month.
The man was arrested at about 9pm yesterday in the same district where the alleged offence is believed to have taken place.
Police obtained a six-day remand order against him today from the Bachok magistrate's court under Section 117 of the Criminal Procedure Code.
The alleged incident is believed to have occurred on June 29, and investigations are ongoing.
Bachok police chief Superintendent Mohamad Ismail Jamaluddin confirmed the arrest when contacted.
"The case is being investigated under Section 376 of the Penal Code for alleged rape."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bursa Malaysia turns marginally lower at midday on mild profit-taking
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia turned marginally lower at midday today as mild profit-taking emerged in the late morning session, dampening market sentiment.
At 12.30 pm, the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) slipped 0.69 of a point, or 0.04 per cent, to 1,682.24  from Tuesday's close of 1,682.93.
The benchmark index opened 3.05 points higher at 1,685.98 and moved between 1,681.27 and 1,690.57 during the morning trading session.
Market breadth was negative with losers outnumbering gainers 492 to 355, while 519 counters were unchanged, 1,305 untraded, and 12 suspended.
Turnover stood at 1.74 billion units worth RM899.56 million.
In a note today, Apex Securities Bhd said heavyweight-led gains are now at risk of turning into outright profit-taking pressure, particularly among technology and semiconductor-linked stocks.
"As such, the market is likely to turn more defensive in the near-term and await clearer signs of stabilisation in regional chip sentiment before re-engaging.
"On domestic catalysts, Bank Negara Malaysia's overnight policy rate decision on July 9 and the Johor state election on July 11, remain on the radar, but external volatility is likely to dominate price action for the rest of the week," it said.
Among heavyweight stocks, Maybank rose four sen to RM10.96, CIMB gained one sen to RM7.61, Public Bank and Tenaga Nasional were flat at RM4.91 and RM14.30, respectively, while IHH Healthcare dropped nine sen to RM8.29.
Among active stocks, Zetrix AI remained unchanged at 75.5 sen, GIIB and Pentech eased half a sen each to 49.5 sen and 27.5 sen, respectively, and AirAsia X lost four sen to RM1.14, while Dagang NeXchange added one sen to 44 sen.
Among the top gainers, United Plantations garnered 48 sen to RM34.22, Allianz Malaysia gained 28 sen to RM20.88, Kuala Lumpur Kepong advanced 20 sen to RM21.88, Malaysian Pacific Industries climbed 16 sen to RM46.00, while United Malacca was 13 sen higher at RM6.07.
Fraser & Neave led the losers, falling 32 sen to RM29.34, Nestle dipped 28 sen to RM90.52, Petronas Gas lost 18 sen to RM17.62, Press Metal Aluminium shed 17 sen to RM7.50, and Hong Leong Industries slipped 12 sen to RM18.82.
Among the broader indices, the FBM Emas Index edged down 5.93 points to 12,456.86, the FBMT 100 Index eased 5.23 points to 12,287.68, the FBM 70 Index declined 8.53 points to 17,766.07, while the FBM Emas Shariah Index trimmed 18.79 points to 12,265.56, and the FBM ACE Index erased 19.61 points to 4,854.54.
By sector, the Financial Services Index climbed 28.80 points to 19,956.76, the Energy Index improved 2.68 points to 764.01, the Plantation Index rose 63.11 points to 9,104.99, while the Industrial Products and Services Index inched down 1.27 points to 179.93.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: France's Marine Le Pen says she will run for president in 2027, appeal her conviction
Article:
PARIS: French far-right leader Marine Le Pen announced on Tuesday that she will run for president in 2027,  after an appeals court shortened her ban on running for office, even as it upheld her conviction for embezzling EU funds to pay party staff.
She also said she would appeal the conviction. Le Pen immediately launched her campaign website and urged voters to back her, creating an unprecedented situation in France, with a lead candidate heading to the ballot after a guilty verdict for a public-funds-related offence.
"Tonight, I am a candidate in the presidential election," Le Pen said in a prime-time interview on TF1 TV, hours after the ruling.
"The French will have the last word." Le Pen, who is leading in opinion polls, said she was taking Tuesday's judgement to the country's highest court, the Cour de Cassation, which has the effect of suspending the ruling, including its requirement that she wear an electronic ankle tag.
She reaffirmed that she had done nothing wrong.
The 57-year-old's presidential hopes had been in limbo since March 2025, when she received a five-year electoral ban for using money from the European Parliament to pay wages for staff at her anti-immigrant National Rally (RN) party in France.
The RN had started preparing for the possibility that Le Pen's 30-year-old protege, Jordan Bardella, would be its candidate,
Tuesday's judgment made Le Pen ineligible to hold public office for 45 months rather than 60, with 30 suspended.
As the ban has been running since last year's ruling, the required 15-month ban has already been served.
[[nid:1483444]]
The appeal court said that although it had confirmed Le Pen's guilt, it had also taken into account "the voter's freedom of choice, a prerequisite for the expression of democratic suffrage."
The RN leads opinion polls for next April's election. And Le Pen, who has three times failed to win the presidency for the far-right in 15 years at the helm, is gambling that voters can overlook the guilty verdict.
"There is no longer any scenario in which I will not run in 2027," she said defiantly on TF1.
Le Pen over the past months had said she would not run for the presidency if the court put her under electronic monitoring because it would interfere with campaigning and undermine her credibility.
But she told TF1 that her appeal to the Cour de Cassation meant she would not need to wear the electronic tag.
In any case, the Cour de Cassation has previously said that, in case of an appeal, it would try to rule on the Le Pen case before the election.
The RN has become the largest single party in the National Assembly, although France's parliament remains split among three main blocs: the far right, the hard left and the centre.
Greens leader Marine Tondelier said that "in a normal world where the RN had even the slightest shred of morality, (Le Pen) would give up ... because you can't decently stand for election after being convicted of misappropriating public funds."
Le Pen's conviction stems from charges that RN figures misused funds intended for assistants in the European Parliament. In 2025, the judges found she had played a central role in the scheme, a finding she disputed.
Tuesday's ruling confirmed she was guilty of embezzling public money.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: GB Bond gets Bursa's nod to list on ACE Market in Q3
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Penang-based industrial adhesives manufacturer GB Bond Holdings Bhd has secured approval from Bursa Malaysia to list on the ACE Market, with its debut targeted for the third quarter of 2026.
The initial public offering (IPO) comprises 64.3 million new ordinary shares and an offer for sale of 42.88 million existing ordinary shares.
Upon completion of the exercise, GB Bond's enlarged issued share capital will stand at 412.3 million shares.
The company said proceeds from the public issue will primarily be used to support business expansion, including renting a new factory and acquiring machinery and equipment to increase manufacturing capacity.
The funds will also be used to establish a sales office in Vietnam, purchase product formulation equipment, undertake marketing activities, finance working capital requirements and defray listing expenses.
"The planned new factory is expected to support the company's manufacturing capacity expansion for industrial adhesives and sealants.
"The proposed Vietnam office is intended to strengthen GB Bond's customer servicing, order processing and logistics coordination in the market," it said.
Malacca Securities Sdn Bhd is the principal adviser, sponsor, underwriter and placement agent for the IPO.
Managing director Datuk Gooi Ching Koay said the approval marks a key milestone in the company's 25-year corporate journey.
"The proposed listing will give us a stronger platform to expand capacity, strengthen our regional presence and support the next phase of growth," he said in a statement today.
GB Bond manufactures water-based industrial adhesives, emulsion polymers and sealants, serving industries such as paper and packaging, fabric lamination, woodworking, building materials and construction.
The company operates an integrated manufacturing model, producing its own emulsion polymers that are used in the formulation of industrial adhesives and sealants.
For the financial year ended Dec 31, 2024 (FY24), GB Bond posted revenue of RM56.34 million and gross profit of RM21.6 million, translating into a gross profit margin of 38.33 per cent.
The group served more than 1,000 customers during the year, with recurring customers accounting for 85.87 per cent of its customer base.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: S&P Dow Jones puts Indonesia on watch list for market downgrade
Article:
SINGAPORE: S&P Dow Jones Indices warned Indonesia risks a downgrade to frontier from emerging status due to market transparency issues, echoing caution from rival index provider MSCI in the latest setback for the country.
Indonesia has been placed under review by MSCI since January and the risk of a downgrade has tanked an equity market that was once the toast of emerging market fund managers - leaving it down more than 30%, or 35% in dollar terms, for the year so far.
Stocks in Jakarta, the worst-performing major stock market this year, fell 1% amid broader risk-off sentiment on Wednesday.
The warning in January from MSCI spurred a slate of measures from Indonesia, including a policy to double the free float requirement for listed firms from 7.5% to 15%.
S&P DJI said it was continuing to monitor efforts to improve stock ownership transparency, including exchange-led reforms aimed at addressing disclosure concerns and the potential impact on market liquidity.
MSCI last month extended its review of Indonesian equities to November, giving regulators more time to pursue reforms but leaving the threat of a downgrade hanging over a market that narrowly retained its emerging-market status.
"If circumstances worsen, S&P DJI may consider implementing special treatment for Indonesian securities," it said. "If these matters remain unresolved one calendar year from the date special measures are introduced, Indonesia's market classification will be assessed at the next annual review."
Indonesia, along with Turkey, were added to a watchlist for potential inclusion in next year's market review process, S&P DJI said in a statement late on Tuesday. Nigeria was also on the list for a potential upgrade to frontier from standalone status.
"What investors are looking for now is not more warnings, but evidence that market accessibility, liquidity and governance are improving in practice," said Mohit Mirpuri, fund manager at SGMC Capital. "There is probably some headline fatigue among investors as well."
FOCUS REMAINS ON MSCI'S NOVEMBER DEADLINE
Jeffrey Hendrik, the chief executive of Indonesia's stock exchange (IDX), said the bourse will have constructive discussions with the related S&P Dow Jones Indices to investigate the concerns raised and understand the issues that are of concern in the evaluation process.
"Together with the OJK (Indonesia's financial regulator) and all stakeholders, the IDX will continue to make various efforts to address existing concerns," said Hendrik, who took over the exchange last month.
Last month, MSCI described the reforms as a "step in the right direction," but said the consistent implementation and sustained effects of these measures are needed.
Angus Mackintosh, ASEAN specialist at Aletheia Capital, said the MSCI deadline in November is more important, noting that if Indonesia satisfies MSCI, it would be unlikely that the other index providers would downgrade.
"The IDX and OJK need to address the other outstanding issues to put the whole issue to bed," Mackintosh said. "A focus on enacting punishment for those responsible for stock manipulation would be a good thing."
Indonesia has faced a string of setbacks this year, with Moody's and Fitch lowering their outlooks on the country's sovereign credit ratings to negative, citing concerns over policy credibility.
Investor jitters over President Prabowo Subianto's spending plans have added to the pressure, casting a shadow over the investment outlook for the $1.4 trillion economy. Foreign investors have net sold over $4 billion worth of Indonesian stocks this year.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Loke: Carey Island port to be developed in phases over 20 years
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The proposed Carey Island port project, aimed at transforming Port Klang into a regional megaport, could take up to 20 years to complete as it will be developed in phases, Transport Minister Anthony Loke said.
He said the project's long timeline reflected its scale, with planning dating back several years.
"Completion will take years. We will build it in phases," he told reporters after opening the Asean Ports and Logistics Exhibition and Conference 2026 here today.
Loke said the feasibility study for the project alone had taken two to three years to complete, with the cabinet approving it in 2018.
"I remember in 2018 we approved the feasibility study. So the feasibility study alone took two to three years," he said.
